differentials.name: Adenomatous polyp of stomach
differentials.raw: Adenomatous polyp of stomach (10-20%) =  true neoplasm with malignant potential (10-80%, increasing with size)

differentials.name: Eosinophilic polyp
differentials.raw: Eosinophilic polyp (peripheral eosinophilia, linitis plastica appearance, small bowel changes)

context: Age:   increasing incidence with age; M÷F = 2÷1 Histo:   intestinal metaplasia (common) + marked cellular atypism Associated with:   Gardner syndrome; coexistent with gastric carcinoma in 35% Location:   more commonly in antrum (antrum spared in Gardner syndrome) √  broad-based elliptical / mushroom-shaped polyp ± pedicle; usually solitary √  usually > 2 cm in diameter (in 80%) √  smooth / irregular lobulated contour √  trabeculated / lobulated slightly irregular contour Cx:   malignant transformation DDx:
